narrative: In FY2023-24, Tata and SAIL split leadership by indicator, but Tata had the more balanced profile. Tata ranked 1st for value-chain environmental assessment at 74% and tied 1st for sustainable sourcing at 100%, while SAIL led MSME/small-producer sourcing at 29.28% and tied 1st for sustainable sourcing at 100%. JSW led local sourcing at 65%, just ahead of Tata’s 64%, but it remained weak on value-chain environmental assessment at 0%. Jindal Stainless remained behind on sustainable sourcing at 90%, but its 16% value-chain environmental assessment showed an emerging supplier ESG programme. Tata’s disclosures show RSCP-based supplier maturity assessment and a 4-step Sustainable Procurement Framework planned for FY2025 buying decisions, giving it a stronger assurance pathway than peers with high headline values but thinner assessment coverage.
